title: Vehicle Factory for Parking Lot
description:
Create a Simple Factory for a parking lot system:
- Vehicle interface with getSize() and getHourlyRate()
- Car: MEDIUM size, $20/hr
- Truck: LARGE size, $50/hr
- Motorcycle: SMALL size, $10/hr
- VehicleFactory.create(type) returns the right vehicle
- Use enum for type safety
- Demo creating vehicles and printing their details
hints: Start with an enum VehicleType { CAR, TRUCK, MOTORCYCLE }
Vehicle interface has two methods: getSize() and getHourlyRate()
Factory uses switch/when on the enum to return the right concrete class

title: Notification System with Registry Factory
description:
Create a Registry-based Factory for notifications:
- Notification interface with send(message) and getChannel()
- Email, SMS, Push, WhatsApp implementations
- NotificationRegistry using Map<Enum, Supplier> for lazy creation
- Support dynamic registration of new types at runtime
- Demo: register a custom 'Telegram' notification without modifying factory code
hints: Use EnumMap<NotificationType, Supplier<Notification>> for the registry
Supplier.get() creates the object lazily -- only when requested

title: Payment Gateway with Factory Method + Strategy
description:
Design a payment system combining Factory and Strategy:
- PaymentProcessor interface: validate(), charge(amount), refund(txnId)
- CreditCardProcessor: Luhn validation, card network charging
- UPIProcessor: VPA validation, NPCI API charging
- Abstract PaymentService with factory method createProcessor()
- Each service subclass owns private creation logic (config loading, key management)
- Simple Factory on top to select the right service based on user choice
- Demo the full flow: user selects payment method -> factory picks service -> service creates processor -> processes payment
hints: PaymentService is abstract with createProcessor() as factory method and processPayment() as the template
PaymentServiceFactory.create(type) returns the right PaymentService subclass
This combines: Simple Factory (select service) + Factory Method (create processor) + Strategy (processor interface)
